name: Beef Chow Fun with Black Bean Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 Green bell pepper, seeded and cut into 1-inch cubes
2 teaspoons Rice wine or dry sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the beef into 1/4-inch thick, 1 1/2-inch long, and 1/2-inch wide slices. Place in a bowl and toss with the beef marinade. Set aside.
2. Cover the black beans with water until soft (about 5 minutes). Drain and rinse off excess salt. Mash together with garlic and ginger into a coarse paste; set aside.
3. Separate the strands of rice noodles. Set aside.
4. Over high heat, preheat the wok until hot. Add half the oil and swirl to coat the sides. When the oil is hot, add the beef; toss until the beef loses its redness (about 30-to-45 seconds). Remove to a bowl; set aside.
5. Add remaining oil to the wok. While warming, add the black bean mixture; stir-fry until fragrant (about 10 seconds).
6. Over high heat, add onions and peppers; stir-fry until they begin to soften but remain crisp (about 1 minute).
7. Add the rice noodles; stir-fry to coat the strands with seasoned oil (about 30 seconds).
8. Add white pepper, sugar, and soy sauce; toss until evenly distributed.
9. Return the beef; toss a few turns to mix in.
10. Transfer to a serving platter. Serve immediately.
